patch.value: Windows packed-extension install regression exceeds the generic Vitest timeout

PR #573 Windows regression run 29511033166 job 87664537351 failed only because the real packed npm extension installation test exceeded Vitest's generic 30-second timeout (about 36.2 seconds). The operation itself completed; the test needs an explicit install-aware timeout that preserves the real end-to-end assertion. Duplicate-safe all-status searches reviewed closed Windows/npm lineage pm-xaib, pm-2kkl, pm-gf6f, pm-arax, and pm-0fhw; those items describe completed historical failures, not this current timing regression.
The packed-extension install integration test passes on Windows Node 24 without weakening its real npm-install assertions; focused local test and the hosted Windows regression job pass

message: Track current PR #573 Windows packed-install timeout after all-status duplicate search

value.text: Implementation evidence: the failing hosted run completed the underlying npm work but exceeded Vitest's generic 30-second ceiling at about 36.2 seconds. The existing real pack/install/doctor assertions are unchanged; only this integration test now declares a 60-second external-install budget. Focused execution passes locally in 1.48 seconds.

value.text: Full-corpus proof: all 292 files / 5,746 tests pass at exact 100/100/100/100 with the real packed npm extension test preserved and included. Hosted Windows confirmation remains pending on the next PR head, so this issue remains actively in progress.

patch.value: Fixed with an install-aware 60-second Vitest timeout; hosted Windows Node 24 regression and full exact-coverage corpus pass without weakening the packed npm install test.
